description_long: This object is a flintlock-style shotgun with a wooden body and a dark metal barrel. The stock is ornately carved, featuring a curved grip and a decorative flourish near the top. The barrel is relatively short and cylindrical, with a smooth, unvented surface and no visible ribbing. The trigger guard is large and angular, providing ample space for a finger and adding to the antique aesthetic. A prominent hammer and frizzen mechanism are visible above the trigger, characteristic of early firearm designs. The overall appearance suggests a historical or decorative weapon, likely intended for display or period reenactment.
description: A flintlock-style shotgun with a carved wooden stock, dark metal barrel, and prominent hammer mechanism, featuring ornate details and an angular trigger guard.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, ornate wooden stock, and prominent hammer mechanism.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the circular barrel opening and decorative carvings along the top of the stock.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the curved grip, trigger guard, and the smooth, unvented barrel.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the stock and the alignment of the barrel.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, smooth and unvented with no visible ribbing
attribute_trigger_guard_design: large, angular trigger guard with a squared shape and ample finger space
